data.description:

An announcement from Mulsanne Group Holding Limited ( (HK:1817) ) is now available.

Mulsanne Group Holding Limited has disclosed the current composition of its board of directors, confirming one executive director who is also the chief executive officer, a chairman serving as a non-executive director, several additional non-executive directors, and three independent non-executive directors. The company has also detailed the makeup of its three key board committees, assigning independent non-executive directors to chair the audit and remuneration committees and the non-executive chairman to lead the nomination committee, underlining an emphasis on independent oversight and formalized governance practices.

These committee assignments indicate that independent directors play a central role in supervising financial reporting and executive pay, while the board chairman oversees director nominations and succession matters. For shareholders and other stakeholders, the clarified structure provides greater transparency into how oversight responsibilities are distributed across the board, which may help bolster confidence in the company’s governance and decision-making processes.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1817) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.29 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mulsanne Group Holding Limited stock, see the HK:1817 Stock Forecast page.

More about Mulsanne Group Holding Limited

Mulsanne Group Holding Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1817. It is governed by a board comprising an executive director who serves as chief executive officer, non-executive directors, and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Huaibei GreenGold Industry Investment Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2450) ) is now available.

Huaibei GreenGold Industry Investment Co., Ltd., a PRC-incorporated industrial investment group listed in Hong Kong, derives its revenue from manufacturing and related industrial operations. The company’s performance is influenced by its capital structure and financing costs, which weigh on profitability despite a positive gross profit.

For 2025, the group reported revenue of RMB219.0 million, down 7.3% year on year, and a widened net loss attributable to equity shareholders of RMB28.8 million, reflecting rising impairments and weaker margins. Gross profit margin fell sharply from 35.5% to 26.7%, while basic loss per share increased to RMB0.11, underscoring mounting pressure on earnings and signaling ongoing challenges for shareholders amid high finance costs and asset write-downs.

The company targets growth in its core operations while managing financing costs and asset efficiency across its portfolio of subsidiaries.

Green Energy Group Limited has announced a series of boardroom changes effective 26 March 2026, including the resignation of executive director Luo Xian Ping due to other business commitments. The latest update is out from Bilibili, Inc. Class Z ( (HK:9626) ).

Bilibili Inc. has granted 949,277 restricted share units to 102 employees under its Second Amended and Restated 2018 Share Incentive Plan, equal to about 0.23% of its total share capital on a one-share-one-vote basis. The RSUs, granted at no purchase price and based on a market price of HK$182.30 per Class Z share at the grant date, will vest between March 2027 and March 2032 and are subject to clawback provisions for misconduct or termination for cause.

The company says the awards are intended to align employee interests with those of shareholders, strengthen loyalty and retention, and support long-term value creation without imposing additional performance targets. The grants fall within existing scheme limits, do not trigger shareholder approval requirements, and exclude directors, executives, and substantial shareholders, highlighting a focus on incentivizing a broader base of staff while maintaining dilution and governance safeguards.

The latest update is out from Beijing Sports and Entertainment Industry Group ( (HK:1803) ).

Beijing Sports and Entertainment Industry Group plans to amend its articles of association to align with the latest Hong Kong listing and regulatory requirements, including rule changes effective 1 July 2025. The revisions are designed to modernize its corporate governance framework by explicitly enabling electronic voting at general meetings, permitting hybrid and fully electronic meetings, and simplifying notification procedures for documents published on company and exchange websites.

The proposed changes, which also include consequential and housekeeping amendments, will require approval by a special resolution at the company’s forthcoming annual general meeting. A circular outlining the full details of the amendments, along with the AGM notice and proxy form, will be sent to shareholders, signaling an operational shift towards more flexible, technology-enabled shareholder engagement and compliance with evolving market standards.

The latest announcement is out from Da Ming International Holdings Limited ( (HK:1090) ).

Da Ming International Holdings Limited has formalized the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, detailing its composition, meeting procedures, and governance framework to strengthen board oversight. The committee, made up predominantly of independent non-executive directors and including at least one member of a different gender, is tasked with reviewing the board’s structure, size, and diversity, maintaining a skills matrix, and identifying suitably qualified candidates for directorships.

The Nomination Committee will meet at least annually, with the company secretary acting as secretary and maintaining minutes that are circulated to both committee and board members. Xiabuxiabu Catering Management (China) Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0520) ) has shared an announcement.

Xiabuxiabu Catering Management reported a sharp revenue decline of 20.3% in 2025 to RMB3.79 billion, alongside a narrowed but still substantial annual loss attributable to shareholders of RMB300.5 million. The group opened 57 new restaurants but closed 109 underperforming outlets, reflecting a strategic shift toward pruning loss-making sites and realigning locations with its value-for-money positioning amid intense industry competition and more cautious consumer spending.

As of year-end, the company operated 755 Xiabuxiabu and 131 Coucou restaurants in Mainland China, plus a small number of overseas stores, giving it significant scale in a catering market that is recovering only moderately. Management expects that ongoing portfolio adjustments and efficiency measures will help improve restaurant profitability and bolster its position as industry consolidation accelerates, as weaker operators exit and leading brands explore leaner formats and selective overseas expansion.

data.title: Shenzhen International Earnings Slip in 2025 but Comprehensive Income Surges on FX Gains
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Shenzhen International Holdings ( (HK:0152) ).

Shenzhen International Holdings reported 2025 revenue of HK$16.35 billion, up from HK$15.57 billion a year earlier, but saw gross profit edge down to HK$3.44 billion as costs rose. Operating profit slipped slightly to HK$5.10 billion, and earnings attributable to ordinary shareholders fell to HK$2.25 billion, with basic earnings per share declining to HK$0.93.

Profit for the year dropped to HK$2.94 billion from HK$3.69 billion, reflecting lower contributions from associates and higher tax charges, although net finance costs improved. Despite the earnings decline, total comprehensive income surged to HK$5.79 billion, driven largely by favourable foreign exchange translation effects, which may soften the perceived impact of weaker core profitability for investors and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0152) stock is a Hold with a HK$9.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shenzhen International Holdings stock, see the HK:0152 Stock Forecast page.

Shenzhen International Holdings is an infrastructure investment and operation group, primarily engaged in logistics, toll roads and related transportation services on the Chinese mainland and in Hong Kong. The company focuses on operating and developing transportation hubs and logistics networks that support regional trade and economic activity.

Transtech Optelecom Science Holdings Limited ( (HK:9963) ) has issued an update.

Transtech Optelecom Science Holdings Limited has announced the current composition of its board of directors, led by chairman He Xingfu and chief executive officer Zou Li Ming alongside three other executive directors and three independent non-executive directors. The company also detailed the membership of its three key board committees—audit, nomination and remuneration—clarifying leadership and oversight responsibilities, which underscores its corporate governance structure and may provide greater transparency and assurance to shareholders.

The audit committee is chaired by independent non-executive director Leong Chew Kuan, while the nomination committee is overseen by chairman He Xingfu and the remuneration committee is led by independent non-executive director Lau Siu Hang. This mix of executive and independent directors across committees is designed to balance management input with independent oversight, supporting regulatory compliance in Hong Kong and reinforcing governance standards as the company manages its strategic and operational decisions.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Shenzhen International Holdings ( (HK:0152) ) has issued an announcement.

Shenzhen International Holdings has signed a land use right transfer agreement for phase 2, stage 1 of the Reserved Land within its South China Logistics Park redevelopment, covering a 25,008-square-meter residential plot with 145,940 square meters of planned gross floor area and a transfer price of about RMB287 million. The deal marks another milestone in the park’s transformation into a digital economy–oriented, mixed-use district and is expected to support profit realization, cash flow recovery and a closed-loop model of investment, construction, operation and transformation as the group accelerates development of this flagship residential project and moves to secure the remaining reserved land in phases.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0152) stock is a Hold with a HK$9.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shenzhen International Holdings stock, see the HK:0152 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shenzhen International Holdings

Shenzhen International Holdings is an infrastructure and logistics-focused group that has been redeveloping its South China Logistics Park in Longhua, Shenzhen. The company is shifting the park from pure logistics and warehousing toward a mixed-use hub anchored by the digital economy, with complementary high-end commercial and residential projects to capture rising urban land values.
